title: Facility blackout_dates — canonical examples
description: Concrete examples of one-off blackout windows. Consumed by test fixtures + the SDUI blackout editor's presets. Blackouts CASCADE — adding a blackout cancels bookings inside the window.

invariants: starts_at is NOT entirely in the past (retroactive blackouts are meaningless — the observer refuses them)
windows within blackout_dates MAY overlap (redundant safety windows are permitted; adding a blackout inside an existing one is a no-op cascade)

examples.name: Two-day maintenance window
examples.cascade_impact: Every booking with starts_at BETWEEN 2026-08-15 and 2026-08-16 is cancelled with cascade_trigger='facility_blackout'.

examples.name: Morning-only maintenance window
examples.cascade_impact: Bookings on 2026-07-20 with starts_at BETWEEN 06:00 and 10:00 (US Eastern) cancelled.

examples.name: Facility reserved for internal tournament — 3 hours
examples.notes: Common pattern: block off the facility for a tournament WITHOUT making N individual bookings for the tournament matches. When Sports module (Wave 3) lands, the tournament flow will replace this with proper Session bookings.

examples.name: Short-notice weather closure
examples.cascade_impact: Existing next-day bookings cascade-cancel with a mandatory notification (cannot-opt-out).

notes: The blackout_dates array is capped at 100 entries per facility (config('facility.blackouts.max_windows_per_facility')). Tenants with more concurrent blackouts should model them via availability_json recurring-hour changes instead.
Removing a blackout DOES NOT restore cascade-cancelled bookings. Once BookingCancelled has fired, the booking is terminal.

The observer refuses blackout adds whose starts_at + ends_at BOTH lie in the past (retroactive blackouts are meaningless). It PERMITS blackout adds where starts_at is past but ends_at is future (mid-window addition — common for weather closures declared during the day).
